summary: A powerhouse Alpine ’81 second set with Lost Sailor→Saint and a soaring Truckin', captured in crisp soundboard early on but later marred by audience‑filled (AUD) sections.
review: The performance shines – Jerry’s tone is on fire, the Lost Sailor > Saint sequence is inspired, He’s Gone flows into an epic Truckin' and the post‑drum jam into The Other One is a highlight. Recording quality is split: the first half is clean soundboard material, while the latter portion falls back to an audience (AUD) source that sounds thinner and less defined.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical performance (average ~4.6/5) while noting a drop in audio fidelity after the drum segment (recording average ~3.5/5). Weighted combination yields ~4.3 stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Front‑half is crisp soundboard with excellent clarity, high‑end sparkle and natural cymbals; later sections switch to an AUD (audience) filler that sounds thinner, dynamic range a bit squashed and overall less detailed.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is a continuous high‑energy ride – Sailor>Saint kicks off, He’s Gone segues smoothly into an expansive Truckin', followed by a hot jam that leads into Drums/Space and a strong Other One. The transition into Stella Blue feels abrupt but remains musically satisfying.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring tone, inspired playing on Sailor>Saint, hot pre‑drum work and overall fire throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for heavy, unusual fingering during the jam sections (especially on Truckin').
band_member_comments.Bob: Occasional lyrical flubs on Truckin' but his energy lifts the performance; vocal treatment sometimes mars The Other One.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s interplay with Jerry on Lost Sailor highlighted; keyboard texture described as “liquid” and adds depth.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drum segment is a classic ’81 jam; Billy’s whistle intro on Truckin' is singled out as a highlight.
